old_definition: Mixed historical sources (legacy mode23/mechanism + rank2 summary from run_2026_03_25_043610)
new_definition: Single canonical residual-mode run (run_phi2_kappa2_canonical_residual_mode) with fixed source IDs
likely_impact: Historical verdicts blend multiple pipelines and summaries, increasing criterion drift.

old_definition: Primary old rank2 decision subset explicitly no_22K (22 K excluded)
new_definition: Uses all canonical temperatures 4..30 K including 22 K
likely_impact: 22 K is a boundary-anomaly row; inclusion/exclusion shifts stability/correlation narratives.

old_definition: Residual-structure plus mode-proxy correlation summaries (mode2 proxy vs I_peak/kappa)
new_definition: Direct SVD mode-2 explained variance and reconstruction ratio on canonical M(x,T)
likely_impact: Old pipeline weighted descriptor linkage; new pipeline weights spectral energy threshold.
No direct evidence of residual-construction contradiction.
Backbone definition is aligned across old/new canonical runs.
Grid mismatch is not the main discrepancy source.

old_definition: Mode-1 sign fixed by positive median kappa; mode-2 sign handling varied by script/report
new_definition: Mode-1 sign fixed by corr(kappa1,Speak)>0; mode-2 sign fixed by max corr with dPhi1/dx or x*Phi1
likely_impact: Mode-2 interpretive signs and correlations can shift between pipelines.
Equivalent singular values but different left/right vectors; easy to mislabel stability quantities.

old_definition: Mixed: max-abs normalization in decomposition, unit-L2 normalization in deformation closure tests
new_definition: No unit-L2 closure test in final verdict; uses raw correlations and explained variance
likely_impact: Strict closure RMSE criteria and correlation-only criteria can diverge strongly.

old_definition: Mixed per script: projection coefficients, LSQ coefficients, or U*S components
new_definition: Fixed: kappa1=s1*V1, kappa2=s2*V2 from canonical SVD after sign alignment
likely_impact: Coefficient semantics changed between historical summaries.

old_definition: Legacy evidence emphasized RMSE gain + strong mode2-descriptor correlations; no explicit 5% variance floor in rank2_report
likely_impact: Primary verdict flip driver: same matrix has mode2 variance ~0.025 (<0.05) despite strong RMSE gain.

new_definition: SECOND_MODE_PHYSICAL requires (mode2 significant) AND (rank2 improves) AND (phi2 is deformation)
likely_impact: New criterion blocks physical-mode claim when significance threshold fails.

old_definition: Old tests used explicit basis spans {dPhi1/dx, x*Phi1} and higher-order extensions {d2Phi1/dx2, x*dPhi1/dx, x^2*Phi1}
likely_impact: Old strict basis-closure can fail while new correlation-only deformation flag passes.

old_definition: Strict closure thresholds (e.g., cosine>=0.90 and rmse_unit<=0.02)
likely_impact: Much softer threshold in new verdict favors deformation=YES.

old_definition: Old verdicts blend correlation strength, RMSE gains, and ad hoc stability summaries
new_definition: New verdict hard-gates significance by explained variance, then uses RMSE/correlation as secondary gates
likely_impact: Metric-priority change explains conflicting conclusions on same data.

old_definition: Legacy rank2 table reports mode2_stability values matching mode-1 LOO from residual_mode_stability.csv
new_definition: New run does not use that legacy stability field for significance/physicality
likely_impact: Labeling mismatch inflated confidence in old mode-2 stability claims.
